title: chore(deps): update dependency fixtures to ~=4.1.0

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *renovate[bot]*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
New changes are detected. LGTM label has been removed.
The fixtures version update is unhappy, @cevich any idea what to do here?

> @umohnani8 thanks for stopping the merge, that would have been "bad".
> 
> > any idea what to do here?
> 
> I think maybe we can simply ignore this (and any other non-security related updates)? My understanding (maybe wrong) is podman-py isn't shipped anywhere that's supported (RHEL/CentOS/Fedora)? Sure it's in PyPi, but that's kind of the wild-west.
> 
> @rhatdan @umohnani8 opinions?

Is the `Test on fedora-38` the thing to be looked into or is there something else that's an issue. We do ship podman-py on fedora, centos-stream and rhel btw.
I guess I should switch this to packit right away for our copr and fedora builds.

> We do ship podman-py on fedora, centos-stream and rhel btw.

Darn.  Thanks Lokesh for correcting me.

> the thing to be looked into

IMHO, no.  We're not obligated to accept renovate's recommendations, we can close the PR and/or ignore it.  The stats provided in the subject are rather informative on this decision:

| Package | Change | Age | Adoption | Passing | Confidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [fixtures](https://togithub.com/testing-cabal/fixtures) | `~=4.0.0` -> `~=4.1.0` | [![age](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/pypi/fixtures/4.1.0/age-slim)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![adoption](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/pypi/fixtures/4.1.0/adoption-slim)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![passing](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/pypi/fixtures/4.1.0/compatibility-slim/4.0.1)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) | [![confidence](https://badges.renovateapi.com/packages/pypi/fixtures/4.1.0/confidence-slim/4.0.1)](https://docs.renovatebot.com/merge-confidence/) |

> I guess I should switch this to packit right away for our copr and fedora builds.

Not sure that will solve anything.  The issue here is a bleeding-edge dependency proposal by an automated tool.  It fails/breaks CI.  It's not widely used (yet).  I think we can just close this and ignore it.

@jwhonce wdyt? Is the `fixtures` dependency bump important here? I am still a little unsure on the requirements around dependency bumps in python.
Based on the stats given by renovate, looks like it should be okay to ignore.
@umohnani8 I vote to ignore until there is more uptake on this version

### Renovate Ignore Notification

Because you closed this PR without merging, Renovate will ignore this update (~=4.1.0). You will get a PR once a newer version is released. To ignore this dependency forever, add it to the `ignoreDeps` array of your Renovate config.

If you accidentally closed this PR, or if you changed your mind: rename this PR to get a fresh replacement PR.
